diagrams.description: Clinical Psychology is the integration of science, theory, and clinical practice to understand, prevent, and relieve psychologically based distress and dysfunction. This flowchart illustrates its systematic approach: the ultimate goal is to promote psychological well-being and reduce suffering. This is achieved through core activities—assessment (interviews, testing), diagnosis (DSM/ICD), case formulation, and intervention (psychotherapy, prevention). These activities are guided by major theoretical orientations (cognitive‑behavioral, psychodynamic, humanistic‑existential, systemic, integrative) and informed by empirical research. Clinical work occurs across diverse settings (hospitals, private practice, community mental health) and with various populations, ultimately producing outcomes like symptom reduction, improved quality of life, and evidence‑based practice refinement.
